checks.notes: tests/lib/fixtures.sh: set -uo pipefail intentionally absent (documented: callers control error propagation). mktemp has BSD fallback '-t ceos_test'. hooks/validate-dispatch.sh: uses 'date -u +format' only (not -d; BSD/GNU portable). No stat usage. Line endings: 0 CRLF found in all 3 spot-checked new test files. prompt-injection-protection.sh uses 'find agents -maxdepth 1 -name *.md' NOT ls glob — portable.

fixtures.sh: make_state_json exits 77 on missing jq (SKIP, not crash); require_jq() exits 77 explicitly. validate-dispatch.sh does NOT have require_jq guard at top — when jq is absent, all jq calls fall through with 'jq: command not found' stderr (suppressed via 2>/dev/null || true) and verdict stays 'MISSING' for all stages; hook still exits 0. This is acceptable advisory behavior but means jq-less environments silently produce all-MISSING audit logs — no indication to operator that jq is absent. Not a bug (advisory-only), but an operator experience gap.
checks.minor_gap: hook does not warn operator when jq is absent — all stages silently show MISSING

checks.notes: validate-dispatch.sh: malformed JSON covered (jq fails silently, || true, exits 0 — confirmed by adversarial test case 1). Large JSON: jq loads file normally, exits 0 (case 2). Empty stdin: IFS= read -r -t 1 with 1s timeout + || true — empty produces empty stdin_json, BYPASS_MODE stays 0, proceeds normally. NaN/null dispatched_at: jq -e '// empty' returns non-zero for null — verdict correctly MISSING (case 5). make_state_json with non-JSON override: jq -s '.[0] * .[1]' fails at parse time, exits non-zero — caller will receive garbage exit code, but return code is not explicitly caught. Tests calling it via SCRATCH=$(...) would see empty assignment on failure, which is a mild input-safety gap for callers not checking return.

10 commits on forge/v6.10.0. Wave 2a/D (Layer 1 prose): only 5 SKILL.md + core files. Wave 2a/E (Layer 2 hook): only hooks/, docs/guides/, docs/reference/, state/, skills/check-setup/ — no agent or test files. Wave 2a/G (prompt-injection 11 agents): only agents/*.md + 1 test scenario rewrite — clean. Wave 2b batches: only tests/scenarios/ files plus 1 test scenario modification. No cross-stream contamination detected between tracks. Wave 3/I (final harness) restricted to 5 test scenario files + CHANGELOG. Scope cleanliness: good.

REQ-T2-FALLBACK (abort-lane: docs-only fallback if research confidence LOW) is documented in .forge/phase-4-spec/final/formal-criteria.md:275 and phase-5-tdd. However, the abort-lane is NOT documented in any operator-visible file (docs/guides/dispatch-enforcement.md has no fallback/abort-lane section). Research returned HIGH so the abort-lane was not exercised. The omission is acceptable (internal pipeline design, not user-facing), but an operator reading dispatch-enforcement.md cannot learn the degraded-mode behavior. Wave 2b outputs confirm 0 REWRITE-triggered REQ-T1-A3 fallbacks — all 16 REWRITEs shipped.
checks.gap: abort-lane not documented in operator-visible dispatch-enforcement.md

checks.notes: tests/scenarios-hidden/ has 6 files (v61000 prefix): canonical-byte-identical, doc-count-drift, harness-count-204, minor-version-justified, retire-exit-77, validate-dispatch-adversarial. The adversarial file covers: malformed JSON (case 1), 100KB+ large input (case 2), bypassPermissions stdin (case 3), shell metacharacter stage injection (case 4), null dispatched_at (case 5). Homoglyph scenarios not present — disclosed as deferred to v6.11.0 (T3-ADV-1). Harness SCENARIO_DIR points to tests/scenarios/ only — hidden dir excluded from the 203 total count.

10 commits on branch. Layer 1 prose (D) is a single commit touching 5 SKILL/core files — revertable. Layer 2 hook (E) is a single commit — fully separable. 11-agent prompt-injection batch (G) is a single commit — revertable in one shot. Wave 2b REWRITEs are in 2 separate commits (C batch 1, C+F). Wave 2a cross-cutting (H) is separate. All major feature tracks are in isolated commits with no interleaving. Rollback granularity is good: each track can be reverted without breaking others. Minor note: Wave 3/I (harness) commit includes both test fixes AND CHANGELOG — a CHANGELOG-only revert would require cherry-picking.
checks.minor_gap: CHANGELOG and test fixes bundled in same Wave 3/I commit — splitting requires cherry-pick

top_findings.finding: validate-dispatch.sh silently shows all-MISSING audit when jq is absent — no operator warning emitted. Operator cannot distinguish 'jq absent' from 'no stages dispatched'. Advisory-only hook so no correctness impact, but degrades observability value.

make_state_json() does not explicitly guard against non-JSON override argument — jq parse failure propagates as non-zero exit to caller. Callers not checking return code (e.g. SCRATCH=$(make_state_json garbage)) silently get empty assignment. Existing test callers appear to use valid JSON, so no live failures, but a latent trap.

REQ-T2-FALLBACK abort-lane (docs-only fallback when research confidence LOW) is spec-internal only. It is not documented in the operator-facing docs/guides/dispatch-enforcement.md. Since research returned HIGH confidence and abort was not triggered, this is benign for v6.10.0 but leaves a documentation gap for future operators.
Wave 3/I commit bundles CHANGELOG entry with test logic fixes — cherry-pick required if only CHANGELOG needs reverting post-release. Minor rollback friction.

summary: Robustness is solid. Platform portability is clean (BSD-safe date, LF line endings, find not ls). Error handling in the hook is thorough (all jq calls use 2>/dev/null || true, exit-0 always enforced). Adversarial hidden tests cover 5 failure modes. Commit scope is clean with no cross-track contamination. Two low-severity gaps: (a) jq-absent hook behavior is silently degraded rather than explicitly warned, (b) make_state_json non-JSON input propagates jq exit code without explicit guard. Neither impacts correctness. Rollback path is good — major tracks are single-commit and independently revertable.
